codeReview.summary: Baseline computation is generally implemented, but the on-demand query does not enforce per-metric source resolution, which can double-count or mix provider rows for the same metric/date and produce incorrect baselines.

issues.description: computeBaselinesOnDemand filters by user/metric/date only and does not constrain by resolved source. Since health_data_daily is unique by (user, metric, date, source), users with multiple providers can have multiple rows for the same metric/date; this implementation will aggregate all of them, distorting avg/stddev and downstream deltas. LLD requires source-resolved baseline queries (dashboard-backend-lld.md line 722: `AND source = $4  -- resolved source per metric`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature-level acceptance criteria did not carry forward the LLD requirement to apply source resolution in baseline queries, making this regression easy to miss despite passing feature tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json (baseline-computation-service) says to query/decrypt health_data_daily rows in the 30-day window but does not mention source filtering; validation-contract VAL-COMP-001/002/003/014 also omit source-resolution assertions, while dashboard-backend-lld.md line 722 requires `AND source = $4`.

summary: Reviewed feature baseline-computation-service (worker session 4fabcb81-9ac7-4e55-8396-4a4a42ee309b, commit a215b84). Found one blocking correctness issue: on-demand baselines do not apply source resolution, so multi-source users can get incorrect baseline statistics.
